decreases in adhesion energy Gc from 5000 hours of hot-dry (90 degrees C, ~1% RH) and hot-humid (90 degrees C, 60% RH) aging.", "direct_quote": "Adhesion testing performed on coupon-level specimens showed decreases in adhesion energy, Gc, from 5000 h of hot-dry (90 degrees C, ~1% RH) and hot-humid (90 degrees C, 60% RH) aging", "fact_category": "measurement", "relevance_score": 0.95, "confidence": 0.95, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": ["EVA", "POE", "EPE", "adhesion energy", "Gc", "5000 hours"]}, {"statement": "POE encapsulant coupons demonstrated the best adhesion stability under thermal aging, followed by EPE then EVA.", "direct_quote": "POE coupons demonstrated the best stability, followed by EPE then EVA", "fact_category": "measurement", "relevance_score": 0.9, "confidence": 0.95, "perspective": "Industry", "entities": ["POE", "EPE", "EVA"]}, {"statement": "For EVA and POE, hot-humid aged coupons experienced a larger decrease in Gc compared to hot-dry due to enhanced hydrolytic degradation.", "direct_quote": "hot-humid aged coupons experienced a larger decrease in Gc due to enhanced hydrolytic degradation", "fact_category": "causal_link", "relevance_score": 0.93, "confidence": 0.93, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": ["EVA", "POE", "hydrolytic degradation", "Gc"]}, {"statement": "Gel content increased (crosslinking) and crystallinity decreased under hot-aerobic (90 degrees C, 22% RH) and hot-anaerobic (90 degrees C, sealed in N2) aging even without UV or crosslinking initiators.", "direct_quote": "increase in gel content (crosslinking) and decrease in crystallinity of the encapsulants under hot-aerobic (90 degrees C, 22% RH) and hot-anaerobic (90 degrees C, sealed in N2 air)", "fact_category": "measurement", "relevance_score": 0.85, "confidence": 0.93, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": ["gel content", "crosslinking", "crystallinity"]}, {"statement": "FTIR-ATR showed insignificant bulk encapsulant degradation under thermal aging alone, demonstrating the critical role of UV and moisture in accelerating degradation.", "direct_quote": "FTIR-attenuated total reflectance analysis showed insignificant encapsulant degradation, demonstrating the critical role of UV and moisture in accelerating degradation", "fact_category": "causal_link", "relevance_score": 0.8, "confidence": 0.92, "perspective": "Methodological", "entities": ["FTIR-ATR", "UV", "moisture"]}], "evidence_summary": "NREL 2025 IEEE JPV study comparing EVA/POE/EPE adhesion under 5000 h thermal aging. Hot-humid (60% RH) causes larger Gc drops than hot-dry (~1% RH) via hydrolytic degradation. POE most stable. Critical role of moisture in adhesion loss."}, {"source_url": "https://www.osti.gov/biblio/1579796", "source_title": "Adhesion and debonding kinetics of photovoltaic encapsulation in moist environments", "source_type": "journal_article", "source_quality": 0.87, "overall_relevance": 0.82, "year": 2016, "authors": ["Novoa F.D.", "Miller D.C.", "Dauskardt R.H."], "venue": "Progress in Photovoltaics", "doi": "", "atomic_facts": [{"statement": "EVA debond energy decreased from 2.15 to 1.75 kJ/m2 when temperature was raised from 25 to 50 degrees C.", "direct_quote": "debond energy decreased from 2.15 to 1.75 kJ m-2 in poly(ethylene-co-vinyl acetate) (EVA)", "fact_category": "measurement", "relevance_score": 0.91, "confidence": 0.95, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": ["EVA", "debond energy", "2.15 kJ/m2", "1.75 kJ/m2"]}, {"statement": "PVB debond energy dropped from 0.67 to 0.52 kJ/m2 between 20 and 40 degrees C.", "direct_quote": "from 0.67 to 0.52 kJ m-2 in polyvinyl butyral when T increased from 20 to 40 degrees C", "fact_category": "measurement", "relevance_score": 0.84, "confidence": 0.95, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": ["PVB", "debond energy"]}, {"statement": "EVA debond growth rates increased up to 1000-fold with small 10 degree C temperature and 15% RH increases.", "direct_quote": "debond growth rates of EVA increased up to 1000-fold with small increases of T (10 degrees C) and RH (15%)", "fact_category": "measurement", "relevance_score": 0.93, "confidence": 0.95, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": ["EVA", "debond growth rate", "1000-fold"]}], "evidence_summary": "Stanford/NREL mechanics-based study: EVA debond growth rates show extreme sensitivity to T/RH (1000x increase with 10 degrees C/15% RH). Foundational kinetics model for moisture-driven adhesion loss."}]}
